lecture_context.lecture_title: Lecture 16: Vision and Language — Multi-Modal Foundation Models

unit.name: Flamingo training, few-shot behavior, and benchmark shift
unit.description: Explains Flamingo’s interleaved image-text training and how multimodal models expanded evaluation beyond classification.
unit.summary: Flamingo’s training uses long interleaved image-text sequences rather than isolated image-caption pairs [ts=2131s]. A masking scheme ensures that when generating the description for a particular image, the model attends only to that image’s visual features, even though it sees a longer context of images and descriptions [ts=2184s]. This supports applications such as multi-turn dialogue about an image, where a user can ask follow-up questions and the model inherits reasoning capabilities from the underlying language model [ts=2233s] [ts=2257s]. It also supports multi-image reasoning and in-context learning: users can provide examples of image-caption or image-question-answer pairs and ask the model to continue the pattern on a new image [ts=2306s] [ts=2320s]. The lecturer clarifies that giving examples is few-shot learning, while removing those examples would make the task zero-shot [ts=2397s]. Flamingo’s results helped shift the field from reporting mostly classification benchmarks to reporting any visual understanding task that can be framed as question answering [ts=2450s] [ts=2471s].

key_points.text: Flamingo trains on interleaved image-text sequences rather than single isolated captions.
A masking scheme restricts each generated description to attend to the corresponding image features.
Multi-turn dialogue becomes possible because the model combines visual grounding with language-model reasoning.
Flamingo can reason over multiple images and answer questions about what they have in common.
In-context visual learning uses example image-text or image-QA pairs to induce behavior on a new image.
Visual understanding benchmarks expanded because many skills can be framed as question answering.

kp_catalog.name: Interleaved image-text training and masking
kp_catalog.description: Flamingo trains on long interleaved image-text sequences while masking attention so each description attends to the correct image.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because interleaved image-text training and masking clarifies flamingo trains on long interleaved image-text sequences while masking attention so each description attends to the correct image, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: In-context and few-shot multimodal reasoning
kp_catalog.description: A multimodal language model can infer new visual task behavior from example image-text or image-QA demonstrations in the prompt.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because in-context and few-shot multimodal reasoning clarifies a multimodal language model can infer new visual task behavior from example image-text or image-QA demonstrations in the prompt, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Benchmark shift to broad visual question answering
kp_catalog.description: As multimodal language models improved, evaluation broadened from image classification to many visual understanding tasks framed as QA.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because benchmark shift to broad visual question answering clarifies as multimodal language models improved, evaluation broadened from image classification to many visual understanding tasks framed as QA, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
